facts: ERA5 reanalysis 1959-2023 at 0.25°/6-hourly; canonical splits train≤2018 / val=2019 / test=2020.
hypotheses: The temporal split reflects a natural shift (climate-change baseline drift) rather than mere convenience.
ML weather models generalize from ERA5 training to an IFS-analysis test (a cross-source shift within the same climate).
intuitions: WeatherBench 2 leaderboard 2024-25 (GenCast, Aurora); ECMWF AIFS deployment; cross-reanalysis (MERRA-2, JRA-3Q).
unknowns: ERA5 reanalysis blends observation with an assimilation model, so which kind of generalization an OOD test on ERA5 measures cannot be cleanly determined.
The 0.25°/6-hour grid is a fixed commitment; the underlying physics resolution cannot be recovered from ERA5 itself.
